      On the way back to the van we saw a
(15) paper factory, worked by power from a big
water wheel.
summer rises & falls
[hand-drawn diagram of paper-making mechanism with labels:]
   A
granite tub with
mush of water/rags &[?]?
                              3 hammers to
                              a tub

                         rice hammers
            cogs connecting
            to large water-wheel.
The paper was lovely – it had
flowers & leaves felt in it (see previous
page) John bought a quotation on politics)
      We saw the church (Eve bumped her head on
the lintel on the way out) & ate pypes in the church
Square.
      We drove on. On several journeys we
have seen lacy-looking mountain peaks in
the distance. They are called the Dentelles de
Mont mirail. They are very pretty.
      We drove on to the Abbaye Notre Dame
de Sénanque, famous for its lavender. There
are lots of lavender fields all around & figs
have been planted too. As we arrived a man
was using an amazing tractor to harvest the
lavender right next to the monastery. It was a
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn diagram of a paper-making mechanism (not a route map): shows a hammer labelled 'A', a granite tub with 'mush of water/rags', '3 hammers to a tub', a 'long pole with wooden bumps to rice hammers', and 'cogs connecting to large water-wheel'. This is a mechanical sketch, not a geographic route sketch.
pages.notes: Page continues from a previous page (begins mid-sentence). The diagram is a mechanical sketch of a water-powered paper mill, not a geographic route map — route_sketch is set true to flag the drawn element but it depicts machinery. 'pypes' is transcribed verbatim (possibly 'crepes' misread, but the diary clearly writes 'pypes'). 'Mont mirail' is written as two words in the diary; the postcard on the facing page spells it 'Montmirail'. 'figs' could be 'figs' (the fruit) or possibly 'Figs' as a planted crop — transcribed as written. No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner. Year not determinable from this page alone; volume section context suggests 2005–2008.

bit like a vine tractor. Under the driver's
seat, chains went down backwards,
obliquely to the ground with to hook claws on
them.      ////goo°   They gathered up the
bunch of  goo°°  lavender & cut it off  in
a bow shape the same shape as over the top of
                              the bowl.
[glued-in postcard: three photos of rocky mountain scenery, labelled "Dentelles de Montmirail En Provence"]
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '6' visible in the top-right corner. The text contains a cluster of small doodle/sketch marks ('////goo°' and 'goo°°') that appear to be the writer's visual representation of bunches of lavender being gathered — transcribed as best-effort renderings of these symbol clusters. 'to hook claws' may be 'hook claws' with 'to' as a preposition, or possibly '[two] hook claws' — transcribed verbatim. A glued-in postcard occupies the lower two-thirds of the page showing three photographs of the Dentelles de Montmirail rock formations in Provence, with printed caption 'Dentelles de Montmirail / En Provence'. No year determinable from this page; volume context suggests 2005–2008.
